title: ugetulda
text:
ugetulda Get the address of the Oracle logon data area (LDA) of the first Oracle logon path. unsigned char* ugetulda (); Copy Parameters None Return Values The address of the logon data area (LDA) of the first Oracle logon path, if any. Use Oracle only, uora NN (your currently used Oracle driver). Description ugetulda returns a pointer to the LDA for the first Oracle logon path, if any. You can use this pointer to call Oracle OCI functions in your user 3GL This function can be used only when an Oracle driver is available to your application. Moreover, the returned pointer is valid only when your application has made one, and only one, connection to Oracle. To check whether the returned pointer points to a valid LDA, you should first call the function ugetuopenflag . In previous versions of Uniface, it was allowed to call ugetulda before a connection to Oracle was made, and use the returned address to set the LDA (obtained by a call to orlon ), so the Oracle driver would not have to log on to the database. In Uniface V8, this is no longer supported. If you write 3GL code using ugetulda and ugetuopenflag , you must link your shared library with the relevant Oracle driver shared library, because these functions are located in the Oracle driver, rather than in the Uniface core libraries. For example, if you are using the U6.1 Oracle connector, you need to link your 3GL code with the uora61 shared library (or .lib file, on Microsoft Windows) in the Uniface lib directory. CAUTION: You should take extreme care when calling Oracle OCI functions in user 3GL, because these will work on the same database being accessed by the Uniface application. Any interference with the regular Oracle I/O, especially inside a transaction, is likely to lead to unexpected results. Example: ugetulda and ugetuopenflag The following 3GL code drops the table FOOBAR from the current Oracle database. Functionally, this might not be the best example, but it is simple and serves to illustrate the use of ugetulda and ugetuopenflag . The routine DROPTABLE can be called, using perform or activate , once the application has logged on to Oracle. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> #include <ocidem.h> EXPORT void DROPTABLE () { unsigned char *pflg; Lda_Def *plda; Cda_Def cda; pflg = ugetuopenflag(); if ( *pflg != 1 ) { printf("No valid LDA
"); return; } plda = (Lda_Def*)ugetulda(); if ( oopen(&cda, plda, NULL, -1, -1, NULL, -1) ) { printf("Open cursor failed
"); return; } if ( oparse(&cda, (text *)"DROP TABLE FOOBAR", -1, 0, 2) ) { printf("Drop table failed, Oracle error = %d
", cda.rc); return; } printf("Drop table OK.
"); } Copy Below is the command to link the shared library on Solaris 64-bits. Note the explicit linking of the U4.2 Oracle driver; this was tested with Oracle 8.1.7. Explain this code cc -g -KPIC -xtarget=ultra -xarch=v9 -G \ -I 3gl/include \ -I $ORACLE_HOME/rdbms/demo \ -o lib/libdroptable.so \ -L./lib -luora42 \ droptable.c Copy Related Links ugetuopenflag Get the address of the flag that specifies the validity of the Oracle Logon Data Area (LDA).
